text: [Music]
Paris is situated in northern France on
the banks of the river sand. With a
population of over 12 million people,
the French capital is at the heart of
the Il France region.
Paris earned her name the city of light
during the age of enlightenment when
many visionary ideas were born. It is a
light that has remained undimemed and
which now attracts 42 million visitors a
year making Paris the most visited city
in all the world.
Paris is a city easily explored by
metro, taxi, and bicycle. But her charms
are best found on foot. Her attractions
are never far apart. And in between,
well, merely walking your streets is to
wander through picture
[Music]
postcards. The engine room of Paris is
Laance. This modern business district
filled with light and art is testimony
that Paris is designed for living even
when at
work. From the futuristic Grand at La
Def France, the sixmile long historic
axis of Paris leads us back into
France's grand
past. The ark de triumph built by
Napoleon rises from the center of Pl and
offers commanding views of the 12 grand
avenues which radiate outwards like a
[Music]
star from the ark the triumph. The shant
continues along the historic
axis. This grand avenue is where
Parisian come to dine, shop, enjoy the
theater and to celebrate life.
Gradually opening into formal gardens
and majestic buildings, the Shanza
emerges into the largest square in
Paris, the
[Music]
Plaston. Just a short stroll away is the
world's greatest treasure house of art,
the Miz Deuv.
Once a 14th century palace, today the
Louver is the most visited art gallery
in the
world. With over 35,000 artworks, her
most famous residents are at the Mona
Lisa and the Venus Demilo. But be
warned, this collection of priceless
artworks and antiquities is simply too
vast to explore in just one day.
[Music]
Not far from the Miz delu stands the
Sant Pompidu displaying the largest
collection of modern art in
Europe. The regions are still debating
whether this radical design is the
vision of a madman or a
[Music]
genius. NRAAM Cathedral is situated on
Il de la city, a natural island in the
river.
Completed in 1345, this Gothic
masterpiece with her flying buttresses
and gargoyles has split center stage to
some of the defining moments of French
history and literature.
[Music]
Parisians see it as their duty to enjoy
life to its fullest.
The Luxmbro gardens with its grand basan
fruit groves and over 100 statues and
fountains is the ideal place to grab a
deck chair and play the Parisian at
rest. Nothing says Paris like the Eiffel
Tower. The iron lady can be seen from
all over the city. However, nothing can
prepare you for the moment when you
first stand at her feet or the views
from the top that you will hold dear for
a lifetime.
Looking north, the city rises into the
hillside neighborhood of MMA. Once the
artistic center of Paris or twisting
streets and narrow lanes were at one
time the home of Picasso, Delhi and Van
Go. They are the perfect place to lose
yourself and discover those special
Parisian moments. But you can never be
lost for long in my mark. As long as you
head upwards, you'll eventually come to
a gleaming white crown, Sakrea
[Music]
Basilica. Leave the bustling city behind
and step through the gates of the Saint
Pelashes. Here, amid the quiet world of
bird song and introspection, you can pay
your respects to Jim Morrison, Oscar
Wild, and some of the world's greatest
minds from whom Paris is now forever
home. Once a day's coach journey from
central Paris, the Palace of Versailles
is now easy halfhour train ride away.
This magnificent 17th century chateau
welcomes everyone from heads of state to
[Laughter]
backpackers. The river runs right
through the heart of Paris, creating a
natural divide between her famous left
and right
[Music]
banks. Of her 37 bridges, the P
Alexander is considered the most ornate,
while the graceful Pondar offers some
incredible vistas of the
city. Artists and photographers gather
here to capture the light while lovers
attach padlocks to the railings as if to
say, "This is Paris. Now we are Paris,
too. All
[Music]
right.
